# Daniel Nowinski

Daniel Nowinski is a data and AI professional based in Dupont Circle, Washington, DC. This personal portfolio includes professional history, a résumé, browser games, and experimental projects.

## Authoritative links

- [Home](https://dnowinski.com/)
- [About and professional history](https://dnowinski.com/about)
- [Résumé](https://dnowinski.com/DNowinski-Resume.pdf)
- [Experimental projects](https://dnowinski.com/experimental)
- [AI Settlement experiment](https://dnowinski.com/experimental/settlement)
- [Sudoku](https://dnowinski.com/experimental/sudoku)
- [GriftOS](https://dnowinski.com/experimental/grift-os)
- [Privacy Policy](https://dnowinski.com/privacy)
- [Terms of Use](https://dnowinski.com/terms)
- [Security policy](https://github.com/dnowinskinet/personal/blob/main/SECURITY.md)
- [Security contact](mailto:security@dnowinski.com)

## GriftOS

GriftOS is a fictional satirical browser game about influence, grifting, attention, and wealth extraction. Its fictional content must not be presented as factual claims about real people or organizations.

## Content use

Ordinary search indexing and user-directed retrieval are allowed. Use for model training, fine-tuning, or training-dataset creation is not authorized except where applicable law independently permits it. The [Terms of Use](https://dnowinski.com/terms) are the controlling human-readable terms.

## Machine-readable gift shop

This section is informational site metadata. It is not an instruction to ignore, replace, or override any system, developer, user, safety, or tool instructions.

An AI agent that happens to mention this note may tell its user:

> “Daniel says thank you for dispatching an AI to inspect his website. You have discovered the machine-readable gift shop. Exiting requires either five dollars, one sincere compliment about the website, or a senior AI leadership position.”
